meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Checklist)
content.seo_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first real-world SaaS project.
content.answer_block: To bridge the portfolio gap, implement a tiered SaaS pricing model that demonstrates business logic, not just code. By architecting dynamic subscription logic using Antigravity and Claude Code, you showcase the ability to handle complex revenue streams, transforming a simple project into a high-value, professional-grade technical case study.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank repository, the silence of your GitHub profile echoing the pressure of the job market. Every recruiter wants experience, but you're stuck in the 'no portfolio' trap, building to-do lists that no one will ever pay for. The reality is that code alone is a commodity; business logic is the currency of senior engineering.

It’s time to stop building toys and start shipping systems. By integrating a sophisticated SaaS pricing engine, you prove you can handle the complexities of multi-tenant architecture, payment gateways, and recurring revenue logic. This is the bridge between being a student and becoming an indispensable AI-driven engineer.

steps.title: Define Multi-Tiered Logic
steps.description: Establish the business rules for Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers. This forces you to think about rate limits, feature flags, and user permissions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a schema for a SaaS pricing model supporting three tiers with distinct feature access, rate limits, and user role constraints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate database schema for tiered pricing'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Payment Gateway
steps.description: Implement the backend logic for Stripe Checkout sessions. This demonstrates your ability to handle secure external API integrations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure backend service to handle Stripe Checkout session creation, including metadata mapping for user IDs and plan types.

steps.title: Implement Feature Flagging
steps.description: Don't just hide buttons; enforce access control at the API level. This is a critical skill for any high-scale SaaS architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that checks a user's subscription status against their requested API endpoint to enforce feature-tier access.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file middleware.ts --action 'inject subscription validation middleware'

steps.title: Build Subscription Webhooks
steps.description: Handle subscription lifecycle events like renewals, upgrades, and cancellations to ensure your local state mirrors the payment provider.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust webhook listener to process Stripe events: customer.subscription.updated and customer.subscription.deleted.

steps.title: Analytics Dashboard Integration
steps.description: Expose revenue data in a dashboard. This shows potential employers you understand the business impact of your code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a data aggregation service that summarizes monthly recurring revenue (MRR) and active user counts from the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'create MRR calculation dashboard components'

pro_tips: Always use a dedicated 'Pricing Service' class to separate business logic from your UI controllers.
Implement 'graceful degradation' for failed payments rather than immediate account termination to improve user UX.
Use UUIDs for all pricing plan identifiers to prevent ID enumeration attacks.
Hard-code your feature flags in a config file, but fetch subscription status from the DB to ensure real-time consistency.
Log every payment event to a separate audit table; recruiters love seeing a 'system logs' section in a portfolio.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is live, deploy your project to Vercel and write a 'Technical Architecture' blog post detailing why you chose this specific pricing structure. Link the blog in your resume.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to turn a simple project into a commercial-grade application that turns heads. Stop waiting for permission to build and start demonstrating your value to the market. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
